title: Never Enchant at Level 30 For Feather Falling

scenes.clipper_notes: First person opening an enchantment table with the bookshelves (studio world) around it and hovering over the enchant level 30 option with the mouse
No clip for this one
Show feather falling diamond boots on the player in the first person menu like equipped

scenes.script_line: Because that's normally the best way
scenes.clipper_notes: 4a and 4b. Show my player standing infront of the enchanting setup from scene #1 'talking to the camera' first as 4a. Then 4b is the other angle showing the camera my player was talking to.

Show the first person inventory with my player having full enchanted diamond armor equipped with good enchants on each piece like unbreaking 3, mending, and protection 4, this clip is hovering the mouse over all 4 from top to bottom

scenes.script_line: but it's just not for feather falling specifically.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player destroying the bookshelves from the earlier scene with an axe like they're removing the enchanting level of the thing

scenes.script_line: If you do a level 22 enchant,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show boots in the enchanting table with a level 22 enchant (you need to have 11 bookshelves around it to get exactly 22)
Show timelapse of '44%' being built in blocks in studio world
Show enchanting diamond boots and getting feather falling 4 on them. its a 44% chance so should only take a couple tries. (enchant then hover over boots)
Show '2.7%' chance being built with blocks in studio world with like dirt blocks showing its bad
Show enchanting diamond boots at level 30 enchant but then not getting it. (Enchant then hover over boots showing the enchant)

scenes.script_line: Which is kind of a crazy difference,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show a further away view of the enchanting table setup with the bookshelves
No clip
Show on a board in a studio world enchant books in item frames (about 40)

scenes.script_line: has a range of enchant levels where they can be rolled
scenes.clipper_notes: Show in a studio world a 'range' being built. Like a shape with the min / max and notches at the end. For this first one have it be gray concrete.

scenes.script_line: and another smaller range where you have the highest chance of rolling it.
scenes.clipper_notes: Similar to #15, build another range within the other one out of green concrete, like showing its a subset / subrange of the other representing the optimal part of the range.
Show my character and the camera block infront of the ranges from 15 and 16 and the camera block that Im talking to is visible.

scenes.script_line: if you want boots with feather falling 4 and protection 4,
scenes.clipper_notes: Will have 18a, 18b, 18c, 18d. First 18a is first person inventory completely empty. then b is now there's diamond boots in the middle. then c is now those diamond boots have feather falling 4, then d is now it has feather falling 4 and protection 4.

scenes.script_line: you're better off getting feather falling
scenes.clipper_notes: Show TrackEntity of my character falling from a long ways up but surviving (with enchanted boots representing the feather falling)
Show 22 being built with emeralds in a studio world
Show the number '11' being built in bookshelves
Show first person crafting another pair of diamond boots
Show first person in the survival hotbar with 30 levels (you can xp give yourself in levels with the command to get 30)

scenes.script_line: for protection and then anviling them together.
scenes.clipper_notes: So they'll be diamond boots with feather falling 4 and boots with prot 4 in an anvil. At the first part of the scene have the mouse over protection 4 and then move your mouse to confirm the anvil, ending up with the combined boots

scenes.script_line: But this weirdly affects other enchants too.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player in full diamond armor being shot by a ton of skeletons which are off camera.
Show my player walking towards the camera (If you can have it so they're still stuck with a ton of arrows from getting shot)

scenes.script_line: you want to use level 21 (which no one cares about)
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player running up to an enchanting table with bookshelves around it (about 10 or 11) like they're about to enchant something

scenes.script_line: But what's actually interesting is that
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player talking to the camera with the camera block visible infront of the scene from #27
Will have 29a, 29b, 29c. first show a sharp 5 book, then eff 5 book, then thorns 3 book.
Show 3 enchant books in item frames (representing the unnamed others)

scenes.script_line: its actually impossible to directly get these from a level 30 enchant,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show having about 10 unenchanted diamond swords and enchanting each one at level 30 enchant table as if they're trying to get sharpness 5 directly.

scenes.script_line: I thought I was just getting unlucky this whole time...
scenes.clipper_notes: No clip for this one
